original: Vanellope Wilkins, born with her heart outside her body, is the first UK baby to survive birth and operations to reinsert her heart
"Toy Story", the first feature-length film created completely using computer-generated imagery, directed by John Lasseter and starringTom HanksandTim Allen, is released
Guion Bluford, Col USAF, NASA astronaut (STS 8, 61A, 39, 53) 1st African American in space, born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
(Geraldine) "Jerrie" Mock, American airplane pilot, and 1st woman to fly solo around the world (1964), born in Newark, Ohio (d. 2014)
First interracial TV kiss (Star Trek - Captain Kirk and Uhura)
Two Chicago TV stations are hijacked by an unknown pirate dressed as Max Headroom
Mel Brooks' first film, "The Producers" starring Zero Mostel andGene Wilderhas a disastrous premiere and is almost shelved - later goes on to win an Oscar and achieve cult status
Fred Rogersof PBS "Mr Rogers, Neighborhood" presents a sweater to Smithsonian Institution
US PresidentBarack Obamaposthumously awards the Presidential Medal of Freedom to pioneering computer scientist and Navy AdmiralGrace Hopper
20-year-oldMike Tysonbecomes youngest heavyweight champion in boxing history when he stops titleholderTrevor Berbickin round 2 at Las Vegas Hilton to earn the WBC title
Edmonton OilerWayne Gretzkybecomes the 13th NHLer to score 500 goals
Lionel Messiof FC Barcelona sets a new goal scoring record in La Liga of 253 goals
Norwegian,Magnus Carlsendefeats Viswanathan Anand to win the 2013 World Chess Championship
Film "The Addams Family" starring Raul Julia,Anjelica HustonandChristina Ricciis released
"Wicked" (Part 1), a film musical, opens starringAriana Grande,Cynthia Erivo, and Jonathan Bailey, based on the stage musical andL. Frank Baum's novel "The Wonderful Wizard of Oz"
Leaning Tower of Pisa's tilt reduced by 4 cm in 20 year project to stabilise it
Mount St Helens in Washington erupts
Discovery of uninhabited Juan Fernández Islands off Chile by Spanish sailor Juan Fernández - later famous home of marooned sailor Alexander Selkirk (inspired Robinson Crusoe story)
Spirit Cave Mummy, North America's oldest at 10,600 years old, is returned to the Fallon Paiute Shoshone tribe for reburial after DNA sequencing proved he is an ancestor of contemporary tribes
"Miss Goodall and the Wild Chimpanzees" broadcast on CBS, watched by 25 million, bringsJane Goodallto international attention
Angela Merkelbecomes the first female Chancellor of Germany
1st snowmobile patent granted to Carl Eliason (Sayner, Wisconsin)
"Santa Claus Is Comin' to Town" 1st heard on Eddie Cantor's show
Regular Concorde supersonic jet passenger service between New York City's JFK airport and Europe begins (Air France from Paris; British Airways from London)
Isolation of a single gene is announced by scientists at Harvard University
Conjunction of Venus, Mars, Uranus, Neptune, Saturn, and the Moon
Queen Elizabeth IIorders the ringing of the bell at Westminster Abbey forJohn F. Kennedyafter his assassination, first time the bells are rung for someone outside the royal family
Film musical "Meet Me In St Louis", starringJudy Garland, and directed by Vincente Minnelli, premieres at Loew’s State Theater on Washington Avenue in St. Louis, Missouri
RCA Records makes its best investment, paying $35,000 to Sun Records forElvis Presley's contract
The Beatles release "The Beatles" (White Album), their only double album
XVI Summer Olympic Games open at the MCG in Melbourne, Australia; first to be staged in Southern Hemisphere and Oceania; first to be held outside Europe and North America
Flying boat "China Clipper" takes off from Alameda, California, carrying 100,000 pieces of mail on the first trans-Pacific airmail flight
Tarzan of the Apes, Fictional character from the famous novel of the same name by author Edgar Rice Burroughs, "born" in the West African jungle
Terry Gilliam, comedy author-animator (Monty Python), born in Minneapolis, Minnesota
In his second title defense,Muhammad Aliscores 12th-round KO ofFloyd Pattersonat Las Vegas Convention Center to retain his world heavyweight boxing championship
YouTube hosts the largest ever live broadcast, YouTube Live
Californian man Craig Coley pardoned for a double murder after serving 39 years by GovernorJerry Brown. Longest prison term to be overturned in the state.
Comic strip "Cathy" by Cathy Guisewite debuts
'Vegas Golden Knights' is revealed as the Las Vegas NHL expansion team's name
SD Charger Dan Fouts passes for 6 touchdowns vs Oakland (55-21)
5th Rugby World Cup Final, Stadium Australia, Sydney: Fly-halfJonny Wilkinsonlands winning drop goal in extra time as England beats Australia, 20-17
24-year-old RussianDaniil Medvedevwins ATP World Tour Finals tennis title beatingDominic Thiemof Austria 4-6, 7-6 (7-2), 6-4 in London
Swiss tennis starMartina Hingisbeats AmericanLindsay Davenport7–5, 6–4, 4–6, 6–2 in the WTA Tour Championship final at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Yugoslavian tennis starMonica SelesbeatsMartina Navratilovafor the second consecutive year to win WTA Tour Championship 7–5, 6–3, 6–1 in NYC; her 3rd straight end of season title
In Pittsburgh, Penguins set NHL record for scoring fastest 5 goals (2:07) in 10-4 win over St. Louis Blues
St. Louis HawkBob Pettitsets NBA record, hitting 19 of 19 free throws
Sandra Volker swims world record 50m backstroke (28.57 sec)
Miles DavisQuintet debuts a jazz concert at Carnegie Hall, NYC
Orchestral work "Bolero" byMaurice Ravelfirst performed publicly in Paris, France
The Beatles release their second album "With the Beatles" in UK
Lake Buena Vista Club opens
US 63rd manned space mission STS 33 (Discovery 9) launches into orbit
Columbia returns to Kennedy Space Center via Kelly AFB, Texas
Columbia moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating of STS 61-C
Portuguese navigatorVasco da Gamarounds the Cape of Good Hope on the first voyage from Europe to reach India
Construction begins on the Simplon tunnel through the Alps, linking Italy and Switzerland; it becomes the world's longest railroad tunnel
Largest swearing-in ceremony, 38,648 immigrants become US citizens
US ends 22 year travel ban to China
Humane Society (US) forms in Washington, D.C.
Test Cricket debut ofGordon Greenidgeand Viv Richards, at Bangalore
